stemHtml:
A single-engined piston aeroplane is flying at an altitude of 8000 ft. Given the following information, if the engine fails at this altitude, what should be the expected net glide ground distance with an elevation of 250 ft?

L/D: 9.1:1
TAS: 140 kt
Headwind: 23 kt
Assume 1 NM = 6080 ft

Still Air Distance = (Height difference / Net gradient) x 100
Ground Distance = Still Air Distance x (GS / TAS)
